cards.title: Why metal coins have a distinct smell
cards.body: When you touch iron or copper coins, that metallic smell isn't actually the metal itself. Instead, it is a human body odor. Rubbing these metals triggers a rapid chemical reaction with the oils on your skin, instantly breaking them down into organic compounds like 1-octen-3-one. This volatile molecule evaporates off your skin, producing the sharp, metallic scent we mistakenly associate with the clean metal.

cards.title: You are mostly made of empty space
cards.body: If you removed all the empty space from the atoms making up every human on Earth, the entire global population could fit inside a single sugar cube. Atoms are 99.9999999 percent empty space. If the nucleus of an atom were the size of a marble, the electrons orbiting it would be about the size of dust motes located over half a mile away.

cards.title: The vertebrate that can live for centuries
cards.body: Deep in the freezing waters of the North Atlantic, the Greenland shark moves at a glacial pace, growing just one centimeter per year. This slow-motion lifestyle gives them an astonishing lifespan. By radiocarbon dating the proteins in their eyes, scientists discovered these sharks routinely live for hundreds of years, making them the longest-lived vertebrates on Earth.

cards.title: Birds navigate using quantum physics
cards.body: Many migratory birds can perceive Earth’s magnetic field thanks to a protein in their eyes called cryptochrome. When light hits this protein, it triggers a quantum reaction, creating a pair of entangled electrons. This entangled pair is highly sensitive to the angle of Earth's magnetic field, essentially giving birds a quantum compass that allows them to "see" magnetic lines as patterns of light and shade.

cards.title: The black hole that sings to its galaxy
cards.body: While space is mostly a vacuum where sound cannot travel, some parts are filled with dense gas. In the Perseus galaxy cluster, immense clouds of hot gas provide a medium for sound waves generated by a supermassive black hole. The black hole's ripples translate to a real acoustic hum, though it is far too deep for human ears—vibrating at 57 octaves below middle C.

cards.title: Pufferfish don't make their own poison
cards.body: The deadly neurotoxin found in pufferfish, tetrodotoxin, is up to 1,200 times more poisonous than cyanide. Yet, pufferfish cannot produce this toxin on their own. Instead, they acquire it by harboring symbiotic bacteria in their tissues. If a pufferfish is raised in a sterile environment and fed a controlled diet, it grows up completely non-toxic and safe to eat.

cards.title: The planet where the Sun rises in the west
cards.body: Venus rotates on its axis in the opposite direction of almost every other planet in our solar system. If you could stand on its scorched, volcanic surface, you would watch the Sun rise in the west and set in the east. Even stranger, Venus spins so slowly that a single rotation takes 243 Earth days, which is longer than its orbital year of 225 Earth days.

cards.title: The placebo effect works on animals too
cards.body: The placebo effect isn't exclusive to humans. In clinical veterinary trials, dogs and horses given inactive sugar pills often show real signs of recovery. Animals can't consciously expect a drug to work, but they respond to classical conditioning—associating medical routines with feeling better—and to the subtle, positive changes in how their owners treat them during a study.

cards.title: The crushing weight of the deep ocean
cards.body: At the bottom of the Mariana Trench, nearly seven miles down, the water pressure is immense. At about 1,000 times atmospheric pressure, it exerts a force of eight tons per square inch. This is equivalent to having an elephant stand on your thumb. Only highly specialized organisms can survive here, as their cell membranes are structurally adapted to prevent collapsing under this weight.

cards.title: Lightning is hotter than the Sun's surface
cards.body: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to temperatures reaching 30,000 kelvins (about 53,000 degrees Fahrenheit). This is roughly five times hotter than the visible surface of the Sun. This extreme, sudden rise in temperature causes the surrounding air to violently expand and contract, creating the explosive shockwave we hear as thunder.

quizzes.question: What actually causes the metallic smell when you touch copper or iron coins?
options: Particles of metal rubbing off on your skin
A chemical reaction breaking down oils on your skin
The oxidation of moisture in the air
Bacteria living on the metal's surface
quizzes.explanation: The metallic smell is actually 1-octen-3-one, a volatile compound produced when your skin oils rapidly react with iron or copper.

quizzes.question: Which protein in a bird's eye allows it to utilize quantum entanglement to detect magnetic fields?
options: Rhodopsin
Cryptochrome
Melanin
Hemoglobin
quizzes.explanation: Cryptochromes are light-sensitive proteins that form radical pairs of entangled electrons, helping birds perceive Earth's magnetic field.

quizzes.question: How do pufferfish acquire their deadly tetrodotoxin poison?
options: By producing it in specialized liver cells
Through symbiotic bacteria in their tissues
By eating venomous jellyfish
From volcanic vents on the ocean floor
quizzes.explanation: Pufferfish do not synthesize the poison themselves; they host symbiotic bacteria that produce the tetrodotoxin.

quizzes.question: How do animals experience a placebo-like response during clinical veterinary trials?
options: Through physiological classical conditioning and changes in owner behavior
By understanding the purpose of the pills
Through a purely imaginary response with no biological changes
By mimicking other animals in the clinic
quizzes.explanation: Classical conditioning (associating medical routines with healing) and positive attention from owners trigger real physiological changes.

quizzes.question: How much hotter than the surface of the Sun can a lightning bolt get?
options: About twice as hot
About five times hotter
Roughly the exact same temperature
Only half as hot
quizzes.explanation: Lightning can reach temperatures of 30,000 kelvins, which is about five times hotter than the Sun's surface (around 5,778 kelvins).

guesses.prompt: What percentage of an atom's volume is made up of entirely empty space?
guesses.explanation: Atoms are almost entirely empty space; if you removed it all, the entire human race would fit inside a single sugar cube.

guesses.prompt: What is the estimated maximum lifespan (in years) of the Greenland shark?
guesses.explanation: Greenland sharks grow incredibly slowly in freezing waters, routinely reaching ages of up to 400 years.

guesses.prompt: How many Earth days does it take for Venus to complete just one full rotation on its axis?
guesses.explanation: A day on Venus is longer than its year! It takes 243 Earth days to rotate once, but only 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun.

guesses.prompt: At the bottom of the Mariana Trench, the water pressure is approximately how many times greater than atmospheric pressure at sea level?
guesses.explanation: Water pressure at the trench floor reaches about 1,000 times atmospheric pressure, exerting a crushing 8 tons of force per square inch.
